subtopic: Designing Data-Intensive Applications — Martin Kleppmann (conceptual reference, NOT a pattern catalog)
important_framing: DDIA is a textbook organized by problem area, NOT a pattern catalog. It does not enumerate numbered named patterns the way Azure / microservices.io / Joshi do. To include DDIA material in this registry honestly, treat it as the conceptual foundation behind many of Joshi's patterns and the data-tier patterns in microservices.io.
structure_part_1_foundations: Ch 2: Data Models and Query Languages (relational, document, graph)
Ch 3: Storage and Retrieval (B-trees vs LSM trees, OLTP vs OLAP, column stores)
Ch 4: Encoding and Evolution (Avro, Protobuf, Thrift; schema evolution; dataflow)
structure_part_2_distributed_data: Ch 5: Replication (single-leader, multi-leader, leaderless; sync vs async; read-your-writes, monotonic reads; conflict resolution; CRDTs)
Ch 6: Partitioning (key-range vs hash; rebalancing; routing; secondary indexes — local vs global)
Ch 7: Transactions (ACID; weak isolation levels; snapshot isolation/MVCC; serializability; 2PL; SSI)
Ch 8: Trouble with Distributed Systems (unreliable networks/clocks; partial failures; Byzantine; safety vs liveness)
Ch 9: Consistency and Consensus (linearizability; CAP; ordering guarantees; total order broadcast; 2PC; consensus = Paxos/Raft/ZAB; membership and coordination services)
structure_part_3_derived_data: Ch 10: Batch Processing (MapReduce, dataflow engines, joins, materialized views)
Ch 11: Stream Processing (event logs, change data capture, exactly-once, stream joins, windowing)
Ch 12: The Future of Data Systems (unbundled databases, dataflow, end-to-end argument, ethics)
concepts_referenced_by_other_pattern_sou: Eventual consistency — referenced by Dynamo-style stores
Snapshot isolation / MVCC — Joshi's Versioned Value
Leader-based vs leaderless replication — Joshi's Leader-and-Followers
Hash vs range partitioning — Joshi's Fixed/Key-Range Partitions
Change Data Capture — microservices.io's Transaction Log Tailing
Lambda Architecture (batch + speed layers) — discussed in Ch 12
how_to_use_this_in_the_registry: When building the per-pattern reference pages, cite the relevant DDIA chapter as the canonical conceptual treatment, and Joshi as the named-pattern formulation. Example: Consensus → pattern names 'Paxos' and 'Raft' (Joshi); deep treatment DDIA Ch 9.
